cwe: CWE-770 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling

threat.title: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) via Catastrophic Backtracking
threat.description: ReDoS attacks exploit inefficient regular expressions that exhibit catastrophic backtracking behavior when processing specially crafted input strings. Attackers send malicious payloads that cause regex engines to consume exponential CPU time, leading to application hangs, service disruption, and complete denial of service. This is particularly dangerous in Node.js single-threaded event loop and Python web applications where regex validation is commonly used for email, URL, or input sanitization.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies endpoint using vulnerable regex (e.g., email validation: ^([a-zA-Z0-9_\-\.]+)@([a-zA-Z0-9_\-\.]+)\.([a-zA-Z]{2,5})$)
2. Crafts malicious input with repeating patterns that trigger catastrophic backtracking (e.g., 'aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa!')
3. Submits malicious payload to API endpoint (POST /api/register with email field)
4. Regex engine enters exponential time complexity O(2^n) loop trying all possible match combinations
5. Single request blocks event loop for 30+ seconds, consuming 100% CPU
6. Attacker sends multiple concurrent requests, causing complete application freeze and service outage
prerequisites: Application uses regex with nested quantifiers (e.g., (a+)+, (.*)*)
User-controlled input is passed directly to regex without length limits
No timeout mechanisms for regex execution
Vulnerable patterns in email, URL, or data validation libraries

cve_references.description: validator.js isEmail() function vulnerable to ReDoS via malicious email string with excessive dots and characters
url-regex package vulnerable to ReDoS via malformed URLs with repeating slashes and characters
axios package vulnerable to ReDoS in URL parsing regex via crafted URLs

real_world_breaches.impact: 27-minute global outage affecting millions of sites; CPU exhaustion across edge servers due to single malformed regex in WAF rule
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Deployment of WAF rule containing regex .*(?:.*=.*)$ that caused catastrophic backtracking on specific HTTP requests

real_world_breaches.impact: 34-minute complete site outage; ReDoS in regex used for parsing post content caused 100% CPU consumption
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Regex pattern ^\s*[a-z,\s]+[a-z]+\s*$ triggered catastrophic backtracking on malicious input with 80,000 spaces

bug_bounty_reports.summary: ReDoS in email validation endpoint causing 30-second response times with crafted email addresses

security_research.title: The Impact of Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in Practice
security_research.key_finding: Analyzed 25,000 npm packages and found 10% vulnerable to ReDoS; attack complexity O(2^n) confirmed in real-world patterns

security_research.title: OWASP Regular Expression Denial of Service Prevention Cheat Sheet
security_research.key_finding: ReDoS is preventable through static analysis, input length limits, and timeout mechanisms

mitigations.title: Use Safe Regex Patterns and Static Analysis Tools
mitigations.description: Replace vulnerable regex patterns with safe alternatives that avoid nested quantifiers and catastrophic backtracking. Use static analysis tools like safe-regex and eslint-plugin-security to detect vulnerable patterns during development.
nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle to manage systems is implemented
validation_steps: Run safe-regex on all regex patterns in codebase
Replace vulnerable patterns with safe alternatives
Add ESLint security rules to CI/CD pipeline
Test regex patterns with fuzzing tools (e.g., 'a'.repeat(1000))
Document safe regex patterns in code review guidelines

mitigations.title: Implement Regex Execution Timeouts
mitigations.description: Add timeout mechanisms to regex execution to prevent long-running operations from blocking the event loop. Use libraries like regex-ddos or implement custom timeout wrappers.
iso27001_controls: A.17.2.1 - Availability of information processing facilities
nist_csf: PR.DS-4 - Adequate capacity to ensure availability is maintained
validation_steps: Wrap all user-input regex operations with timeout
Verify timeout errors are logged and monitored
Configure alert thresholds for repeated timeout events

mitigations.title: Enforce Input Length Limits and Rate Limiting
mitigations.description: Implement strict input length validation before regex processing and apply rate limiting to prevent mass ReDoS attacks. Combine with WAF rules to block suspicious patterns.
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Define maximum input lengths for all fields
Implement middleware to enforce length limits before regex
Configure rate limiting (10 requests/min for validation endpoints)
Test with excessive input lengths (e.g., 10KB email string)
Monitor rate limit violations in application logs

indicators: Sudden spike in API response times (>5 seconds) for validation endpoints
100% CPU utilization on single Node.js process or Python worker
Event loop lag exceeding 1000ms in Node.js applications
Repeated timeout errors in application logs
High memory consumption with no corresponding traffic increase
Single IP address making repetitive requests with unusual input patterns

log_queries.query:
// Detect slow regex processing and potential ReDoS attacks
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(15m)
| where TimeTaken > 5000  // Response time > 5 seconds
| where UriStem contains "validate" or UriStem contains "register"
| summarize 
    SlowRequests = count(),
    AvgResponseTime = avg(TimeTaken),
    MaxResponseTime = max(TimeTaken),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(CIp)
    by bin(TimeGenerated, 1m), UriStem
| where SlowRequests > 5
| order by TimeGenerated desc, SlowRequests desc
| project TimeGenerated, UriStem, SlowRequests, AvgResponseTime, MaxResponseTime, UniqueIPs

sequence by host.name, process.pid with maxspan=30s
  [process where event.category == "process" and process.cpu.pct > 0.95]
  [network where http.response.status_code == 200 and event.duration > 5000000000]
| stats count() by host.name, source.ip
| where count > 3

index=app_logs sourcetype=express_access
| eval response_time_ms=response_time*1000
| where response_time_ms > 5000 AND (uri_path="/api/register" OR uri_path="/api/validate")
| stats count as slow_requests, avg(response_time_ms) as avg_ms, max(response_time_ms) as max_ms by client_ip, uri_path
| where slow_requests > 5
| sort -slow_requests
| eval severity=case(
    max_ms > 30000, "CRITICAL",
    max_ms > 10000, "HIGH",
    max_ms > 5000, "MEDIUM"
  )
| table _time, client_ip, uri_path, slow_requests, avg_ms, max_ms, severity

fields @timestamp, @message, @duration
| filter @duration > 5000 and (@message like /validate/ or @message like /register/)
| stats count(*) as slow_requests, avg(@duration) as avg_duration, max(@duration) as max_duration by bin(5m)
| filter slow_requests > 5
| sort @timestamp desc

tools.name: Node.js Event Loop Monitor
tools.usage: const eventLoopMonitor = require('event-loop-monitor'); eventLoopMonitor.on('delay', (delay) => { if (delay > 1000) console.error('Event loop lag detected:', delay); });

tools.name: AppDynamics
tools.usage: Real-time CPU profiling and slow transaction detection

tools.name: New Relic
tools.usage: Set alert conditions for 'Web transaction time > 5s' with threshold 5 occurrences in 5 minutes

tools.name: Azure Application Insights
tools.usage: Create alert rule: 'Server response time > 5000ms' with aggregation 'Average' over 5 minutes

proof_of_concept.description: Demonstrates catastrophic backtracking with vulnerable email regex

safe_alternative.description: Safe regex pattern with linear time complexity
